songContext.summary: A short, hook-driven track that repeatedly tells someone to 'take it easy' — a relaxed, insistent refrain amid brash ad-libs. The singer balances a busy, confident lifestyle with a personal note ('Mera dil vi ni karda ravaun nu' — my heart doesn’t want to leave), mixing nonchalance and mild emotional reluctance. Much of the text consists of repeated slogans and vocal tags rather than extended narrative.
songContext.tone: Laid-back, playful, slightly dismissive and confident with occasional personal softness
notablePhrases: Eni jyada rude kaahto tone krdi
Mera dil vi ni karda ravaun nu

lines.original: Eni jyada rude kaahto tone krdi
lines.literal: You say so many rude things, you change your tone.
lines.natural: You talk so rude and switch your tone.
lines.slangAware: You talk mad rude, then flip your tone on me.
lines.chosen: You talk so rude and switch your tone.
lines.translated: You talk so rude and switch your tone.
lines.note: Transliteration: Eni jyada rude kaahto tone krdi. Conservative literal rendering of 'kaahto' as 'talk/say'.
lines.ambiguity: Moderate — the verb 'kaahto' is unclear in exact form.
lines.selectorReason: Most semantically faithful candidate; preserves the original's accusation about rudeness and changing tone without adding slang not present in source.

lines.original: Tu tan ae kare jive mainu own krdi
lines.literal: You, though, act like you own me.
lines.natural: You act like you own me.
lines.slangAware: You go around acting like you own me.
lines.chosen: You act like you own me.
lines.translated: You act like you own me.
lines.note: Transliteration: Tu tan ae kare jive mainu own krdi.
lines.selectorReason: Clear, idiomatic rendering matching the original's meaning and tone.

lines.literal: Do a little bit of proper behavior, girl.
lines.slangAware: Chill and behave a bit, girl.
lines.note: Transliteration: Thoda jeha karla behave goriye.
lines.selectorReason: Natural, faithful translation of a short admonition; keeps the address 'goriye' as 'girl' conservatively.

lines.original: Enne bebe vi na kre jinne phone krdi
lines.literal: Not even those girls do that who make so many calls.
lines.natural: Not even those girls who call a lot do that.
lines.slangAware: Even those other girls who blow up phones don't do that.
lines.chosen: Not even those girls who call a lot do that.
lines.translated: Not even those girls who call a lot do that.
lines.note: Transliteration: Enne bebe vi na kre jinne phone krdi.
lines.ambiguity: Moderate — 'bebe' likely means 'girls' or 'babes'; phrasing is informal.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative literal choice that keeps sense of comparison (even those girls who call a lot).

lines.original: Paisa fira kuttda ni time kitthe aa
lines.literal: Money moves around, there's no time — where is the time?
lines.natural: Money's moving around; there's no time.
lines.slangAware: Money's on the move, man — who has time?
lines.chosen: Money's moving around; there's no time.
lines.translated: Money's moving around; there's no time.
lines.note: Transliteration: Paisa fira kutdda ni time kitthe aa. Rendered conservatively given transcription uncertainty.
lines.ambiguity: High — fragmented colloquial phrasing; exact sense of 'kutdda ni time kitthe aa' is uncertain.
lines.selectorReason: Keeps literal sense that money is circulating and time is lacking; avoided speculative slang readings.

lines.note: Transliteration: Main bahla busy.
lines.selectorReason: Direct, natural rendering matching the repeated hook about being busy.

lines.note: Transliteration: Busy busy.
lines.selectorReason: Repetition preserved exactly for lyrical effect.

lines.note: Transliteration: Main bahla busy.
lines.selectorReason: Direct, natural rendering matching the repeated hook about being busy.

lines.note: Transliteration: Busy busy.
lines.selectorReason: Repetition preserved exactly for lyrical effect.

lines.note: Transliteration: Oh yaari oh layi ae.
lines.ambiguity: Moderate — 'layi ae' can indicate 'is for' or 'is because of'; meaning slightly ambiguous.
lines.selectorReason: Literal, conservative rendering that preserves possible purposive reading without overinterpreting.

lines.original: Kehda mull lai leya ni thoda easy
lines.literal: What value did you take? Take it a little easy.
lines.natural: What did you take? Take it a little easy.
lines.slangAware: What did you grab? Chill out a bit.
lines.chosen: What did you take? Take it a little easy.
lines.translated: What did you take? Take it a little easy.
lines.note: Transliteration: Kehda mull lai leya ni thoda easy.
lines.ambiguity: Moderate — 'mull' can mean value/price or 'what you took'; kept general.
lines.selectorReason: Combines the two short clauses into clear English while staying close to the literal meaning.

lines.original: Main keha fer vi tu time kahto mangdi rehni aa
lines.literal: Main keha fer vi tu time kahto mangdi rehni aa
lines.natural: I told you, yet you keep asking for time
lines.slangAware: I said it, still you keep asking for my time
lines.chosen: I told you, yet you keep asking for my time
lines.translated: I told you, yet you keep asking for my time
lines.note: Transliteration: Main keha fer vi tu time kahto mangdi rehni aa.
lines.ambiguity: Moderate — 'kahto' transcription uncertain; interpreted as 'for my time/attention'.
lines.selectorReason: Natural English phrasing that matches the original complaint about repeated requests for time.

lines.original: Jadon pata ki rakane sada time aa
lines.literal: Jadon pata ki rakane sada time aa
lines.natural: When you know it's our time
lines.slangAware: When you know it's our time
lines.chosen: When you know it's our time
lines.translated: When you know it's our time
lines.note: Transliteration: Jadon pata ki rakane sada time aa.
lines.ambiguity: Moderate — 'pata ki rakane' transcription slightly unclear; taken as 'when you know'.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative rendering that fits the song context about timing and mutual moments.

lines.original: Haan value tan pata kira kar jatt di ni
lines.literal: Haan value tan pata kira kar jatt di ni
lines.natural: Yeah, at least know a jatt's value
lines.chosen: Yeah, at least know a jatt's value
lines.translated: Yeah, at least know a jatt's value
lines.note: Transliteration: Haan value tan pata kira kar jatt di ni. Glossary: 'jatt' = tough Punjabi man.
lines.selectorReason: Keeps identity term 'jatt' literal while rendering the imperative sense to 'know his worth.'

lines.original: Tere naal di toh puch meri fan aa
lines.literal: Tere naal di toh puch meri fan aa
lines.natural: Ask about being with you — she's my fan
lines.slangAware: Ask her about being with you — she's my fan
lines.chosen: Ask her about being with you — she's my fan
lines.translated: Ask her about being with you — she's my fan
lines.note: Transliteration: Tere naal di toh puch meri fan aa. Chosen conservative interpretation treating 'she' as 'my fan'.
lines.ambiguity: High — pronoun reference is ambiguous; unclear who 'teri/naal' refers to and whether 'meri fan' modifies subject or object.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative, literal reading selected over riskier interpretations; marked low confidence due to pronoun ambiguity.

lines.original: Vajde salute jatt jitho langda ni jive DC
lines.literal: Vajde salute jatt jitho langda ni jive DC
lines.natural: Salutes sound where the jatt passes, like DC
lines.slangAware: Salutes blast where the jatt walks, like DC
lines.chosen: Salutes sound where the jatt passes, like DC
lines.translated: Salutes sound where the jatt passes, like DC
lines.note: Transliteration: Vajde salute jatt jitho langda ni jive DC. 'DC' kept as-is per source.
lines.ambiguity: Moderate — 'DC' appears to be a vocal/tag ad-lib; 'Vajde salute' is stylistic.
lines.selectorReason: Literal, rhythmic translation that preserves the ad‑lib and the simile comparing the jatt's passage to 'DC'.

lines.note: Transliteration: 'DC DC' — vocal/ad‑lib left unchanged.
lines.selectorReason: Ad‑lib/vocal tag; keep as-is for accuracy and musicality.

lines.note: Transliteration: 'Ni jive DC' — 'ni' = 'not'; 'DC' kept as ad‑lib/reference.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative literal rendering: negation + ad‑lib preserved.

lines.note: Transliteration: 'DC DC' — vocal/ad‑lib left unchanged.
lines.selectorReason: Ad‑lib/vocal tag; keep as-is for accuracy and musicality.

lines.original: Oh kam roj da rakane mainu parkhe
lines.literal: Oh kam roj da rakane mainu parkhe
lines.natural: They test me by giving me everyday work.
lines.slangAware: They try me with the daily grind.
lines.chosen: They test me by giving me daily work.
lines.translated: They test me by giving me daily work.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Oh kam roj da rakane mainu parkhe' — 'parkhe' = 'they test/try me'; 'kam roj da' = daily/small tasks.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative literal rendering that keeps the 'testing' sense implied by 'parkhe'.

lines.original: Main vi folan nu fol lava varhke
lines.literal: Main vi folan nu fol lava varhke
lines.natural: I'll also build it up for them.
lines.slangAware: I'll also stack it up for them.
lines.chosen: I'll also build it up for them.
lines.translated: I'll also build it up for them.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Main vi folan nu fol lava varhke' — uncertain phrasing; rendered conservatively as 'build it up'.
lines.ambiguity: High — 'folan' and 'fol lava varhke' are unclear slang/colloquial terms.
lines.selectorReason: Given unclear slang, chose a cautious, non‑speculative paraphrase rather than inventing a meaning.

lines.original: Das ajegi ghdhe de utte tarke
lines.literal: Das ajegi ghdhe de utte tarke
lines.natural: You'll see it on the donkey's back.
lines.slangAware: You'll even see it on the donkey's back.
lines.chosen: You'll see it on the donkey's back.
lines.translated: You'll see it on the donkey's back.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Das ajegi ghdhe de utte tarke' — literal idiom retained to avoid misinterpretation.
lines.ambiguity: High — idiomatic meaning uncertain; literal preserved.
lines.selectorReason: Phrase reads idiomatic/proverbial; preserved literal wording because underlying sense is uncertain.

lines.natural: You'll learn how to spin the cutting wheels.
lines.slangAware: You'll learn to spin the chop wheels.
lines.chosen: You'll learn how to spin the cutting wheels.
lines.translated: You'll learn how to spin the cutting wheels.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Das sikhlegi kattne charkhe' — literal, cautious rendering.
lines.ambiguity: High — 'kattne charkhe' could be literal machinery or a metaphor for a hard lesson.
lines.selectorReason: Kept literal to avoid inferring a specific metaphorical meaning not clearly supported by context.

lines.original: Mera dil vi ni karda ravaun nu
lines.literal: Mera dil vi ni karda ravaun nu
lines.natural: My heart doesn't want to leave either.
lines.slangAware: My heart don't wanna leave either.
lines.chosen: My heart doesn't want to leave either.
lines.translated: My heart doesn't want to leave either.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Mera dil vi ni karda ravaun nu' — straightforward emotional line.
lines.selectorReason: Direct, idiomatic English that matches the Punjabi meaning and song tone.

lines.original: Ni tainu tatto kine keha si banaun nu
lines.literal: Ni tainu tatto kine keha si banaun nu
lines.natural: I told you how many tattoos I'd make for you.
lines.slangAware: I told you how many tats I'd ink you with.
lines.chosen: I told you how many tattoos I'd make for you.
lines.translated: I told you how many tattoos I'd make for you.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Ni tainu tatto kine keha si banaun nu' — 'tatto' read as 'tattoo'; 'banaun nu' = 'to make'.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative, literal reading of loanword 'tatto' and the verb phrase.

lines.original: Tainu kehnda kehda supne ch aun nu
lines.literal: Tainu kehnda kehda supne ch aun nu
lines.natural: I told you which dream to come into.
lines.slangAware: I told you which dream to show up in.
lines.chosen: I told you which dream to come into.
lines.translated: I told you which dream to come into.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Tainu kehnda kehda supne ch aun nu' — kept literal phrasing of 'which dream'.
lines.selectorReason: Literal and conservative rendering preserves original phrasing without overinterpretation.

lines.original: Mainu tu thodi chahidi savaun nu
lines.literal: Mainu tu thodi chahidi savaun nu
lines.natural: I only need a little of you.
lines.slangAware: I only want a little bit of you.
lines.chosen: I only need a little of you.
lines.translated: I only need a little of you.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Mainu tu thodi chahidi savaun nu' — 'thodi' = 'a little'; 'savaun' uncertain.
lines.ambiguity: Moderate — exact role of 'savaun' is unclear; rendered conservatively.
lines.selectorReason: Chooses a simple, natural English phrasing that reflects the probable meaning while flagging uncertainty.

lines.original: Aujle da ikko aa thikana goriye ni
lines.literal: Aujle da ikko aa thikana goriye ni
lines.natural: Aujla has only one place, girl.
lines.slangAware: Aujla's got just one spot, babe.
lines.chosen: Aujla has only one place, girl.
lines.translated: Aujla has only one place, girl.
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Aujle da ikko aa thikana goriye ni' — 'Aujle' = possessive of the artist's name; 'thikana' = place.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative literal rendering; keeps artist reference intact.

lines.note: Transliteration: 'Surrey BC' — likely the city + province (British Columbia).
lines.selectorReason: Context with 'Aujla' place makes province reading most probable; natural English punctuation added.

lines.note: Transliteration: 'BC BC' — left as repeated tag; ambiguous double reading noted.
lines.ambiguity: Moderate — could be province abbreviation (BC) or Punjabi expletive 'BC'; context near 'Surrey' supports province reading.
lines.selectorReason: Kept literal tag while noting alternative slang reading; aligned with nearby 'Surrey, BC' for coherence.

lines.original: Biba akkeya peya main tere shak te
lines.literal: Biba akkeya peya main tere shak te
lines.natural: Girl, I'm caught up in your doubt.
lines.slangAware: Girl, I'm caught up in your doubts.
lines.chosen: Girl, I'm caught up in your doubt.
lines.translated: Girl, I'm caught up in your doubt.
lines.note: transliteration: 'Biba akkeya peya main tere shak te'. 'biba' = girl; conservative rendering chosen for opaque phrase.
lines.ambiguity: High uncertainty in 'akkeya peya' and exact sense of 'shak' (suspicion/hesitation).
lines.selectorReason: Preserves the emotional sense without over-interpreting unclear verbs; flagged low confidence.

lines.original: Main tan sohniye heraan teri akh te
lines.literal: Main tan sohniye heraan teri akh te
lines.natural: Girl, I'm amazed by your eyes.
lines.slangAware: Girl, I'm stunned by your eyes.
lines.chosen: Girl, I'm amazed by your eyes.
lines.translated: Girl, I'm amazed by your eyes.
lines.note: transliteration: 'Main tan sohniye heraan teri akh te'. 'sohniye' kept as affectionate address.
lines.selectorReason: Straightforward literal rendering; fits song's affectionate tone.

lines.original: Kade kare tu sawal lagge takk te
lines.literal: Kade kare tu sawal lagge takk te
lines.slangAware: Sometimes you keep firing questions at me.
lines.note: transliteration: 'Kade kare tu sawal lagge takk te'.
lines.ambiguity: Phrase 'takk te' adds continual sense but exact nuance is light.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative rendering captures repeated questioning implied by the original.

lines.original: Kade puchdi ki banneya ae lak te
lines.literal: Kade puchdi ki banneya ae lak te
lines.natural: Sometimes you ask what I've become, about my style.
lines.slangAware: Sometimes you ask, 'What have I turned into — what's my look?'
lines.chosen: Sometimes you ask what I've become, about my style.
lines.translated: Sometimes you ask what I've become, about my style.
lines.note: transliteration: 'Kade puchdi ki banneya ae lak te'. 'lak' rendered as 'style' conservatively.
lines.ambiguity: Uncertain meaning of 'lak' (interpreted as 'look/style').
lines.selectorReason: Preserves likely intended contrast between personal change and appearance.

lines.original: Mainu ni pasand jadon rehndi balliye ni khiji khiji
lines.literal: Mainu ni pasand jadon rehndi balliye ni khiji khiji
lines.natural: I don't like it when you stay like that, baby — khiji khiji.
lines.slangAware: I ain't fond of you staying like that, baby — khiji khiji.
lines.chosen: I don't like it when you stay like that, baby — khiji khiji.
lines.translated: I don't like it when you stay like that, baby — khiji khiji.
lines.note: transliteration: 'Mainu ni pasand jadon rehndi balliye ni khiji khiji'. 'balliye' = baby/dear; 'khiji' kept as onomatopoeic hook.
lines.ambiguity: Unclear meaning of 'khiji'; treated as vocal ad‑lib.
lines.selectorReason: Keeps affectionate address and preserves ad‑lib without inventing meaning.

lines.note: transliteration: 'Khiji khiji'. Treated as vocal refrain/ad‑lib.
lines.ambiguity: Meaning unknown; likely an onomatopoeic hook.
lines.selectorReason: Literal preservation of vocal hook for lyrical fidelity.

lines.note: transliteration: 'Ni khiji khiji'.
lines.ambiguity: 'ni' functions here as a vocative/filler (like 'hey'); 'khiji' remains ad‑lib.
lines.selectorReason: Maintains vocative feel while keeping the ad‑lib intact.
